Mary's grandmother is referred to only as 'Nonna' and an Aunt Rosa is mentioned as her mother's sister.

demographics.age_rationale: Mary is 28. Her mother's age is not stated, but she is old enough to have an elderly mother ('Nonna, in her eighties') and a younger brother Tony in community college. Mid-50s is plausible for a woman with an 80+ year-old mother.

background.how_met: Mother — raised Mary in Madison from birth
background.why_close: Mary called her 'probably the most important person' in her life (Q2). Her mother taught her to cook, taught her to work hard, and taught her to 'show up even when you don't feel like it.' They share the family shellfish sensitivity — 'My mom has the same thing' (Q6). Her mother hosts Sunday dinner, maintains the Italian cooking tradition passed down from Nonna, and is Mary's most frequent family contact. Mary's quote after the restaurant shellfish incident — 'My mom was like, I told you, always ask twice. She's right' (Q8) — shows an ongoing, advice-giving, protective relationship.

key_traits: Hard-working — Mary credits her work ethic to both parents, and Mom 'works too' on top of hosting family dinners
Skilled home cook — maintains the Italian-American Sunday dinner tradition from Nonna, makes homemade pasta
Protective and practical — 'I told you, always ask twice' about allergens
Devoutly Catholic — attends Mass every Sunday per Mary
Family-centered — hosts Sunday dinners, coordinates with Nonna, involved with parish potluck

inferred_OCEAN.openness: Moderate — embraces the Italian-Midwestern food fusion ('cheddar-crusted bratwurst next to a plate of rigatoni')

personality_mini_profile.communication_style: Inferred from Mary's description: practical, direct, slightly protective. The kind of mother who checks that you've eaten and reminds you to be careful. Her 'I told you, always ask twice' line suggests she communicates in short, authoritative maternal phrases. Likely texts in full sentences. Would double-text if Mary doesn't respond. Probably sends food photos and Mass reminders.

inner_circle.name: Dana

background.how_met: Through the Madison Runners Club. Mary runs along Lake Mendota 3x/week (MWF) with the club, and Dana is a fellow member. Mary says 'There's this woman, Dana, who I run with almost every time' (Q2).
background.years_known: Not precisely stated. Mary joined the running club after a blood pressure scare, which she describes as having led to getting 'consistent about it' (Q8). The timeline suggests roughly 1-2 years.
background.why_close: Dana has graduated from running buddy to 'a real friend, not just a running buddy' (Q4). They've gone on weekend getaways to Door County together — 'hiking, eating cheese curds, hitting up wineries' (Q4). Dana holds Mary accountable for showing up to runs — 'Dana will literally text me if I don't show up' (Q7). Dana also gives Mary life advice, specifically about finances — 'Dana keeps telling me to look into SBA loans for when I'm ready' (Q12 follow-up). Dana's California trip made Mary jealous, suggesting Mary admires Dana's lifestyle and experiences (Q10).

Accountability-oriented — 'will literally text me if I don't show up' (Q7)

Supportive mentor-friend energy — 10 years older, pushes Mary in running and life planning

demographics.ethnicity: Italian — immigrated from Calabria. 'My grandparents came over from Calabria' (Q1)
demographics.location: Madison, WI area (Mary visits her on Wednesdays; attends family Sunday dinners)

background.how_met: Grandmother — lifelong relationship. Mary's earliest cooking memories center on Nonna: 'My nonna made pasta from scratch every weekend and I'd just sit on the kitchen counter watching her roll it out. That's honestly where I got my love of cooking.' (Q1)
background.why_close: Nonna is the origin point of Mary's identity — her love of cooking, her connection to Italian heritage, her Catholic faith, and her sense of family all trace back to this woman. Mary makes a specific effort to visit Nonna on Wednesdays (Q2 follow-up), which is notable given Mary's packed schedule and limited free time. Nonna's cardiac history also directly motivated Mary's health anxiety and running commitment: 'my nonna has had heart stuff, and I don't want to go down that road at twenty-eight' (Q8). Despite slowing down, Nonna 'still wants to make Sunday gravy every week' (Q2), maintaining the tradition that anchors the family.

key_traits: Deeply traditional — immigrated from Calabria, maintained Italian cooking traditions for decades
Devoutly Catholic — attends Mass every single day (Q9)
Persistent and proud — in her eighties and slowing down but 'still wants to make Sunday gravy every week' (Q2)
Matriarch energy — the family's cooking and cultural traditions flow from her
Has had cardiac health issues — 'my nonna has had heart stuff' (Q8)
